stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 60.1 % (within-trial site SD = 5.5). trial site B has 15 observations with mean 57.1 % (within-trial site SD = 6.7). trial site C has 8 observations with mean 37.0 % (within-trial site SD = 9.0). trial site D has 5 observations with mean 57.7 % (within-trial site SD = 9.0). trial site E has 15 observations with mean 63.0 % (within-trial site SD = 6.4). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.0 and 20.0 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site D: 57.6 % (shrunk from raw 57.7). Range [57.5, 57.7] as between-trial site SD varies from 8.0 to 20.0. Pooled mean: 56.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 56.7 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=13.3: B = 0.916.
Interval [57.5, 57.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (57.7) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (56.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
